You can also move at your normal speed while wearing heavy armor." imp11="The penalty is now reduced by 3 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +3." imp15="The penalty is now reduced by 4 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +4."}
::ab[Armor Mastery (Ex)]{next flavor="Text from the fighter class ability." icon=def passive="You gain DR 5/- whenever you are wearing armor or using a shield."}

mounted_fury.name: Mounted Fury
description: ## Mounted Fury
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/58›

Some vigilantes form special bonds with the creatures they use as steeds, considering these loyal and trusted mounts just as much heroes or antiheroes as they might be. Mounted furies often masquerade as anonymous stable hands or humble quartermasters to mask their vigilante activities.
::cskill{gain=ha lose=swim~umd}

::ab[Thorough Change]{icon=down info="A mounted fury must take care when changing from one identity to another, including making any changes necessary to ensure that his mount is not associated with his social identity and vice versa (although if anyone suspects him of being anything other than what he appears to be while in his social identity, he need not make a separate Disguise check for his mount). The mounted fury always requires 1 minute to change identities, regardless of other effects that reduce the time required, and he cannot choose the ‹soctalent/immediate change› or ‹soctalent/quick change› social talents." alter="Dual identity"}

::ab[Mount (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A mounted fury gains the service of a loyal and trusty steed to carry him into battle. This mount functions as a druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion›, using the mounted fury's vigilante level as his effective druid level. The creature must be one that he is capable of riding and is suitable as a mount. A Medium mounted fury can select a ‹companion/camel› or a ‹companion/horse›. A Small mounted fury can select a ‹companion/pony› or a ‹companion/wolf›, but can also select a ‹companion/boar› or a ‹companion/riding »dog› if he is at least 4th level. The GM might approve other animals as suitable mounts. A mounted fury can exchange or replace his mount the same way that a druid exchanges or replaces her animal companion." passive="A mounted fury does not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding his mount. The mount is always considered combat trained and begins play with ‹feat/Light Armor Proficiency› as a bonus feat. A mounted fury's mount does not gain the *share spells* ability." imp3="A mounted fury's bond with his mount is strong, with the pair learning to anticipate each other's moods and moves. The mounted fury's mount shares all his ‹main/teamwork feat«s›, without needing to meet the prerequisites (although while mounted, the mounted fury and his mount do not count as separate creatures for the purpose of these feats)." imp5="The mounted fury's mount benefits from the vigilante's *startling appearance* as if it were the vigilante." special="Should a mounted fury's mount die, the mounted fury may find another mount to serve him after 1 week of mourning. This new mount does not gain the *link* or *devotion* special abilities until the next time the mounted fury gains a level." ability2="A mounted fury can select avenger ‹ability/vigilante talents›, despite not being an avenger. He can also select a teamwork feat for which he qualifies in place of a vigilante talent." imp10="He gains an additional teamwork feat each time he selects a teamwork feat in place of a vigilante talent (if he selected any teamwork feats in place of vigilante talents before 10th level, he gains an additional teamwork feat at 10th level for each time he did so)." order=ap2 replace="Vigilante specialization"}

::ab[Furious Charge (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost-def flavor="A mounted fury learns to make more accurate charge attacks while mounted." passive="The mounted fury and his mount receive a +4 bonus on melee attack rolls on a ‹rule/charge› while mounted (instead of the normal +2). In addition, the mounted fury and his mount don't take any penalty to AC after making a charge attack while mounted." replace="Vigilante talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Mighty Charge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost-melee flavor="A mounted fury learns to make devastating charge attacks while mounted." passive="He doubles the critical threat range of any weapons he wields during a charge while mounted, as well as the critical threat range of the mount's attacks. This increase does not stack with other effects that increase the threat range of weapons." order=pa ability="In addition, the mounted fury can make a free ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/sunder›, or ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver if his charge attack is successful. This free combat maneuver does not provoke an attack of opportunity." replace="Vigilante talent gained at 12th level"}

::ab[Vengeance Strike (Ex)]{l=20 icon=down info="A mounted fury need not be mounted while he studies a target against whom he plans a *vengeance strike,* but he must be mounted when he makes the strike's attack." alter="Vengeance strike"}

mutated_defender.name: Mutated Defender
description: ## Mutated Defender
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/9›

Occasionally a mutation takes hold of the body of a vigilante, but he learns to hide his maladies, usually through alchemical concoctions or magical concealment. Unlike other mutants, who are usually shunned in Alkenstar and other settlements, mutated defenders can appear in public without revealing their mutations - though they can still use them to their full potential in secret.

::ab[Mutant Specialization]{icon=boost-power ability="A mutated defender gains a base attack bonus equal to his vigilante level instead of using those listed in the vigilante's class table. He adds this value as normal to any other base attack bonus gained from other classes or racial Hit Dice." choice="He also selects one ‹template/mutant« deformity› other than *mindless* or *vulnerability.* This deformity magically reverts when the vigilante is in his social identity, as do all mutations from his mutant talents, allowing him to hide his mutation entirely. Unlike with other vigilante talents, the vigilante is entirely unable to use the benefits of his mutant talents while in his social identity." order=ac replace="Vigilante specialization"}

::ab[Mutant Talents]{head icon=power ability="A mutated defender can select from the following mutant talents, in addition to the ‹ability/vigilante talents› normally available to him." alter="Vigilante talent"}

::ab[Mutant Blast (Su)]{next prereq="9th-level vigilante" icon=zap ability="One of the mutated defender's extremities becomes elementally warped, and he can discharge a blast of energy from this limb. This functions as the *elemental ray* bloodline power (from the ‹sorcbloodline/elemental› sorcerer bloodline) and uses his vigilante level as his sorcerer level. The mutated defender chooses the damage type (acid, cold, electricity, or fire) of his mutant blast when selecting this talent." zText_from_the_Elemental_Ray_Bloodline_Power="You can unleash an elemental ray as a @HLstandard_action, targeting any foe within 30 feet as a ranged touch attack. This ray deals 1d6 points of damage of your energy type + 1 for every two sorcerer levels you possess. You can use this ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + your Charisma modifier." order=az}

::ab[Mutant Evolution]{next icon=power ability="The mutated defender gains a 1-point ‹ability/unchained_>evolution<s› from the eidolon list other than ‹unchevolution/climb›, ‹unchevolution/improved natural armor›, ‹unchevolution/mount›, ‹unchevolution/skilled›, or ‹unchevolution/swim›. He must meet any prerequisites as normal, using his vigilante level for prerequisites that require a summoner level. He can select an evolution granting a natural attack without fulfilling the subtype requirement, though he must replace one of his hands with the natural weapon." special="A mutated defender can select this mutant talent multiple times, choosing a different evolution each time."}

::ab[Mutated Lobe (Sp)]{next icon=magic prereq="4th-level vigilante" ability="A mutated defender's brain is enlarged and distended, granting him psychic powers. He can cast ‹spell/detect thoughts› as a spell-like ability, with a caster level equal to his vigilante level." useInc=vigilante~4 useF="4~1"}

psychometrist.name: Psychometrist
description: ## Psychometrist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/59›

Wise vigilantes know that there is power in certain seemingly unremarkable items. Psychometrists aren't spellcasters; instead, they collect strange items or create their own uncanny gadgets that seem to only work for them. Psychometrists often hide their clandestine activities behind the guise of an eccentric peddler, curator, tinker, or artificer.
::cskill{gain=ka~kh lose=kd~ke}
::ab[Occult Awareness]{l=1 icon=power ability="A psychometrist gains ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› as a bonus feat." replace="Social talent gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Implements and Focus Powers (Su)]{l=2 order=ca icon=power choice="A psychometrist learns to use the powers of one ‹ability/implement« school<s› from the ‹class/occultist› class; the conjuration implement school is not available to a psychometrist. This implement school does not allow the psychometrist to cast any spells of that school of magic, though, and he can't use spell trigger or spell completion magic items from any schools without succeeding at the appropriate Use Magic Device check." ability="The psychometrist treats his vigilante level as his occultist level for purposes of determining how his implements' focus powers work, and for determining at what level he may choose certain focus powers. Unlike an occultist, each of a psychometrist's focus powers has its own associated implement or gadget. For example, a psychometrist might own a scabbard implement to use the *legacy weapon* focus power and an implement that is a pair of boots to use the *sudden speed* focus power. The psychometrist must assign mental focus separately to each implement each day, and he doesn't gain resonant powers his implements. Otherwise, this ability acts like the occultist's implements class feature." repeat="The psychometrist learns to use one additional implement school.~0~6" special="Each time a psychometrist learns an implement school, he learns the base focus power from that school, plus one focus power from each implement school he has previously learned (but not from the new school he just learned). The psychometrist can use focus powers only by expending *mental focus* (see below). Unless otherwise noted, the DC for any saving throw against a focus power equals 10 + 1/2 the psychometrist's vigilante level + the psychometrist's Intelligence modifier.~~~The psychometrist can select a school he already knows, and in this case, he gains a total of two new focus powers from that school (instead of learning the base focus power from the new school)." replace="Vigilante talents gained at 2nd, 6th, 12th, and 18th levels"}

::ab[Mental Focus (Su)]{l=2 head icon=power ability="Like an occultist, a psychometrist has *mental focus* he can invest into his implements each day to allow him to utilize abilities tied to them. A psychometrist has a number of points of *mental focus* equal to ‹misc/half› his vigilante level plus his Intelligence modifier."}
:::ab{next title="Mental Focus (Su)" flavor="Relevant text from the occultist class ability." icon=magic action=Ability}

You have a number of points of mental focus; these points refresh each day. You can divide this mental focus between your implements in any way you desire. If an implement is lost or destroyed, the focus invested in it is lost as well, though you still refresh those points of focus normally.
Once mental focus is invested inside an implement, you can expend the mental focus stored in the implement to activate the associated focus powers you know.

You refresh your mental focus once each day after receiving at least 8 hours of sleep. After refreshing your mental focus, you must spend 1 hour preparing your implements and investing them with this power. Mental focus that is not used before the next time you refresh your focus is lost.

You can choose to save generic mental focus inside your own body instead of investing all of it, but expending this focus comes at a higher cost. Any focus power you activate with generic focus costs twice as much mental focus to use (and to maintain, if applicable). You can expend your generic focus through an appropriate implement on any focus power you know.
::ab[Object Reading (Su)]{l=2 head icon=power ability="A psychometrist can read information from items he examines, and gains the occultist's *object reading* ability." replace=Unshakable}

::ab[Object Reading (Su)]{flavor="Text from the occultist class ability." next icon=power ability="You learn how to read information from items you examine. Examining an item in this way requires you to spend 1 minute handling the item. If the item is a magic item, you learn its properties and command word as if you had successfully examined the item using ‹spell/detect magic› and succeeded at a ‹skill/Spellcraft› check. This ability does not reveal whether the item is cursed unless your class level is equal to or greater than the caster level of the item. If the item has any historical significance, you learn one piece of information about its past (as determined by the GM). Finally, if the item was last used no longer than 1 day ago per your class level, you learn one piece of information about the last creature to use the item. This information might be a glimpse of the creature's appearance, a brief vision of what it saw while using the item, or perhaps its emotional state when it last used the item. The GM determines what information is gained in this way. This functions like the ‹skill/psychometry« occult skill unlock›, but doesn't require a skill check and can be used at will."}

::ab[Psychometric Strike (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="A psychometrist gains twice the usual benefits of *vengeance strike* (up to the same maximum) against an opponent who possesses an object that once belonged to the psychometrist." alter="Vengeance strike"}

serial_killer.name: Serial Killer
description: ## Serial Killer
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/71›
Some vigilantes adopt an innocent guise to cloak their acts of brutal murder.

::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning info="A serial killer's vigilante identity must be evil. A serial killer whose vigilante identity becomes nonevil cannot gain new levels as a vigilante but retains all of her class abilities."}

::ab[Vigilante Specialization]{icon=info info="A serial killer must choose the stalker specialization. Her *hidden strike* is considered equivalent to a ‹ability/sneak attack› with the same number of dice for the purpose of meeting prerequisites or using abilities that depend on sneak attack." alter="Vigilante specialization"}

::ab[Thwart Pursuit (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A serial killer adds 1/2 her vigilante level as a bonus on all checks in ‹rule/a »chase<s› and to the DCs of Diplomacy checks to gather information about her and checks to track her or notice her tracks (though not to the DC of the ‹arc-investigator/profiler«'s› expert profiler ability)." replace=Unshakable}

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{l=4 head icon=power ability="A serial killer gains the *studied target* ability of a ‹class/slayer› 3 levels lower, including the ability to study multiple targets simultaneously at the appropriate levels. The serial killer does not provoke attacks of opportunity when attempting a ‹misc/coup de grace› against a studied target." replace="4th- and 14th-level vigilante talents~9th- and 19th-level social talents"}

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{next flavor="Text from the slayer class ability." icon=boost move="You can study an opponent you can see. You then gain a bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks attempted against that opponent, and a bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against it; these bonuses are equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of your slayer level. The DCs of slayer class abilities against that opponent increase by 1 + one-fifth of your slayer level. You can only maintain these bonuses against a certain number of opponents at a time (equal to 1 + one-fifth of your slayer level); these bonuses remain in effect until either the opponent is dead or you study a new target." order=mif immediate="If you deal sneak attack damage to a target, you can study that target immediately, allowing you to apply your studied target bonuses against that target (including to the normal weapon damage roll)." imp7="You can now choose to study a target as a @HLswift_action." free="You can discard this connection to a studied target, allowing you to study another target in its place."}

::ab[Charming (Ex)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A serial killer gains the ‹hex/charm› hex as if she were a witch of her vigilante level, but she can't affect animals. The DC is based on her Charisma, and the ability is nonmagical." replace="Startling appearance"}

::ab[Death Attack (Ex)]{l=6 head icon=power ability="A serial killer gains the ability to make a *death attack* as if she were an ‹pclass/assassin› (DC = 10 + 1/2 her vigilante level + her Charisma modifier)." replace="6th-level vigilante talent"}

::ab[Death Attack (Ex)]{next icon=melee flavor="Text from the assassin prestige class ability." ability="If an assassin studies his victim for 3 rounds and then makes a sneak attack with a melee weapon that successfully deals damage, the sneak attack has the additional effect of possibly either paralyzing or killing the target (assassin's choice). Studying the victim is a @HLstandard_action. The *death attack* fails if the target detects the assassin or recognizes the assassin as an enemy (although the attack might still be a sneak attack if the target is denied his Dexterity bonus to his Armor Class or is flanked). If the victim of such a *death attack* fails a Fortitude save (DC 10 + the assassin's class level + the assassin's Int modifier) against the kill effect, she dies. If the saving throw fails against the paralysis effect, the victim is rendered ‹misc/helpless› and unable to act for 1d6 rounds plus 1 round per level of the assassin. If the victim's saving throw succeeds, the attack is just a normal sneak attack.~~~Once the assassin has completed the 3 rounds of study, he must make the *death attack* within the next 3 rounds. If a *death attack* is attempted and fails (the victim makes her save) or if the assassin does not launch the attack within 3 rounds of completing the study, 3 new rounds of study are required before he can attempt another *death attack.*"}

::ab[Calling Card (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power-boost ability="When a serial killer slays a humanoid with a *coup de grace* or *death attack,* she can leave a telltale token or clue behind identifying this death as her work. The serial killer chooses her calling card when she gains this ability, and the first time she uses it in an area where she has renown (as per the ‹soctalent/renown› social talent), it becomes associated with her killings. After that, once citizens of a new settlement find this calling card, the serial killer immediately establishes the settlement as an area of renown for her vigilante identity without spending additional time to spread tales, and the Intimidate bonus from renown increases by 2. She still must spend time to establish her social identity's renown." replace="7th-level social talent"}

::ab[Grisly Murder (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power-lower ability="A serial killer gains ‹feat/Dreadful Carnage› as a bonus feat, and if she leaves a calling card, the murder is so horrifying that creatures examining the corpse are affected as per ‹spell/nightmare› that night (DC = 10 + 1/2 the serial killer's vigilante level + her Charisma modifier). The *nightmare* affects a number of creatures equal to the serial killer's Charisma modifier, starting with the first creature to examine the corpse." replace="Frightening appearance"}
::ab[Quiet Death (Ex)]{l=12 head icon=power ability="A serial killer gains the *quiet death* class feature as if she were an assassin." replace="12th-level vigilante talent"}

::ab[Quiet Death (Ex)]{next icon=melee flavor="Text from the assassin prestige class ability." ability="Whenever an assassin kills a creature using his *death attack* during a surprise round, he can also make a Stealth check, opposed by Perception checks of those in the vicinity to prevent them from identifying him as the assailant. If successful, those nearby might not even notice that the target is dead for a few moments, allowing the assassin to avoid detection."}
::ab[Swift Death (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power head ability="A serial killer gains the *swift death* class feature as an assassin." replace="Stunning appearance"}

::ab[Swift Death (Ex)]{next head icon=melee flavor="Text from the assassin prestige class ability." ability="Once per day, an assassin can make a *death attack* against a foe without studying the foe beforehand. He must still sneak attack his foe using a melee weapon that deals damage."}

splintersoul.name: Splintersoul
description: ## Splintersoul
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/17›

A splintersoul pushes the boundaries of what it means to have two separate identities. One may be kind and calm while the other is angry and heartless, and the change from one identity to another is sudden and frightening. The splintersoul is torn by conflicting emotions and thoughts, but because of his dual mind, he can master a uniquely disparate set of skills and abilities no normal person should be able to manage.

::ab[Splintered Identity (Ex)]{icon=info-down info="A splintersoul's two identities are even more distant from one another than those of a normal vigilante. He cannot use any of his vigilante talents while in his social identity.~~~However, for the purpose of qualifying for classes, feats, and other abilities, he is eligible if one of his alignments meets the requirements. While in an identity whose alignment is incompatible with an ability, class, or feat, he temporarily loses access to the feat or ability or is treated as an ex-member of the class, as appropriate.~~~For example, a splintersoul with ‹class/barbarian› levels, a lawful-good social identity, and a neutral vigilante identity can't use his vigilante talents or his *rage* class feature while in his social identity, but he regains these abilities and can use them as normal as soon as he changes to his vigilante identity." special="A splintersoul with ‹class/paladin› levels must follow the paladin's normal code of conduct while in a lawful-good identity, but while in an identity with a different alignment, the following changes apply to his code: Willingly committing an evil act (for example, casting a spell with the evil descriptor) still causes the vigilante to become an ex-paladin, but otherwise, he can do whatever else he feels is necessary to uphold the causes of law and good. He should strive to act with honor and uphold the tenets of his faith, but failing to do so is not a violation of his code. At the GM's discretion, other classes or archetypes with similarly strict codes of conduct can also follow a less strict version of a code of conduct while in an identity with an incompatible alignment." alter="Dual identity"}

::ab[Sudden Change (Ex)]{icon=power ability="More adaptive than other vigilantes, a splintersoul must select the ‹soctalent/quick change› social talent at 3rd level and the ‹soctalent/immediate change› social talent at 7th level, ignoring the minimum levels for those talents." replace=Unshakable alter="Social talents gained at 3rd and 7th levels"}

::ab[Surprising Change (Ex)]{icon=power l7="When a splintersoul uses the *immediate change* talent to reveal his vigilante identity and then attacks a foe in the same round, he can use the *startling appearance* ability against that foe as though the foe were unaware of his presence." l11="He can use *frightening appearance* while using this ability." l17="He can use *stunning appearance* while using this ability." special="A splintersoul can use *frightening appearance* and *stunning appearance* only against foes who are unaware of his presence, and he can do so a number of times per day equal to his Charisma modifier. This ability doesn't function against foes who already know the splintersoul's identity." alter="Startling appearance~frightening appearance~stunning appearance"}

teisatsu.name: Teisatsu
description: ## Teisatsu
‹SOURCE Spymaster's Handbook/26›

Teisatsu are specialized vigilantes who focus on infiltrating social scenes and high-society gatherings on behalf of the feuding lords of Minkai. Trained as assassins and spies, they cultivate a suite of personalities and study secret arts that hone their bodies into deadly weapons.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down info="Teisatsu are proficient with all simple weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/kama›, ‹eq-weapon/katana›, ‹eq-weapon/kusarigama›, ‹eq-weapon/nunchaku›, ‹eq-weapon/sai›, ‹eq-weapon/short sword›, ‹eq-weapon/shortbow›, ‹eq-weapon/shuriken›, ‹eq-weapon/siangham›, and ‹eq-weapon/wakizashi›. They are proficient with light and medium armor, but not with shields." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Infiltrator (Ex)]{icon=info info="A teisatsu is required to choose stalker as his specialization." alter="Vigilante specialization"}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=2 icon=power head ability="A teisatsu gains a ki pool, identical to the ‹class/ninja› class feature, using his vigilante level as his effective ninja level." replace="Vigilante talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{next flavor="Relevant text from the ninja class ability." icon=magic-melee order=awp ability="You gain a pool of ki points. The number of points in your ki pool is equal to ‹misc/half› your ninja level + your Charisma modifier." passive="As long as you have at least 1 point in your ki pool, you treat any ‹skill/Acrobatics› skill check made to jump as if you had a running start." imp10="As long as you have at least 1 point in your ki pool, you reduce the DC of Acrobatics skill checks made to jump by 1/2 (although you still can't move farther than your speed allows)." swift="By spending 1 point from your ki pool, you can do one of the following: 1) take one additional attack at your highest attack bonus when making a full attack, 2) increase your speed by 20 feet for one round, or 3) give yourself a +4 insight bonus on Stealth checks for 1 round." special="If the ninja has levels in another class that grants points to a *ki* pool, all of these levels stack to determine the total number of *ki* points in the combined pool, but only one ability score modifier is added to the total. The choice of which score to use is made when the second class ability is gained, and once made, the choice is set. The ninja can now use *ki* points from this pool to power the abilities of every class she possesses that grants a *ki* pool.~~~The ki pool is replenished each morning after 8 hours of rest or meditation; these hours do not need to be consecutive."}

::ab[Teisatsu Talents]{head icon=power ability="A teisatsu selects ‹ability/vigilante talents› as normal. He can't select the ‹vigtalent/rogue talent› vigilante talent. He can, however, select either of the talents below, which are unique to the teisatsu." alter="Vigilante talents"}
::ab[Ki Power (Su)]{next icon=power ability="The teisatsu gains a single ‹ability/ki power<s› of his choice from the list available to the ‹class/unchained monk› class."}

::ab[Shadow Tricks (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="The teisatsu gains a single ‹ability/ninja trick<s› (but not a *master trick*) of his choice. He cannot select a *rogue talent* in place of a *ninja trick* in this manner. If he selects a *ninja trick* labeled as a Primary Sneak Attack Trick, that talent applies to his *hidden strikes* instead of to sneak attacks, and the talent counts as a Hidden Strike Talent for the limitation of applying only one effect to a single *hidden strike.*"}

warlock.name: Warlock
description: ## Warlock
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/60›

Practicing magic in secret, the warlock obscures her arcane scholarship from public view. Her study of these arts allows her to hide her dual nature and enchant her enemies - or simply blast them with furious magical attacks.
::cskill{gain=ka~spc lose=dd~ke~per~sur}

::ab[Skill Ranks per Level]{icon=down info="The warlock gains a number of skill ranks equal to 4 + her Intelligence modifier at each level, instead of 6 + her Intelligence modifier skill ranks." alter="Skill ranks per level"}

::ab[Armor Proficiencies]{icon=down-power ability="A warlock is not proficient with medium armor. Like a ‹class/magus›, she can cast her spells while wearing light armor without incurring the normal arcane spell failure chance." alter="Armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Spellcasting]{icon=learn ability="A warlock casts arcane spells and cantrips as a magus. She prepares spells using a spellbook, choosing them from the 6th-level and lower spells from the ‹main/spells_>sorcerer›/‹main/spells_>wizard› spell list; higher-level sorcerer/wizard spells are not on the warlock's spell list." replace="4th-, 8th-, 10th-, 14th-, and 16th-level vigilante talents"}
::ab[Spellbook]{icon=learn ability="A warlock has a spellbook that functions in the same way as a magus's spellbook but uses the 6th-level and lower spells from the sorcerer/wizard spell list."}

::ab[Mystic Bolts (Su)]{icon=melee-ranged order=an ability="A warlock can sling projectiles of magical energy at will by shooting a bolt or touching her foe. A melee mystic bolt requires the target to be within reach, and a ranged mystic bolt is a ranged attack with a range of 30 feet. A mystic bolt deals points of damage equal to 1d6+‹misc/one-fourth› of her vigilante level." info="The warlock must choose one type of damage for her mystic bolt: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. Attacking with a mystic bolt takes the place of one of the warlock's normal attacks, and she can make a full attack using mystic bolts. The warlock vigilante attacks with mystic bolts as though they were light one-handed weapons, and the bolts can be used for ‹rule/two-weapon fighting› (with each hand creating one mystic bolt) and feats and abilities that apply to weapon attacks (unless they're excluded from that feat, such as with ‹feat/Power Attack›). ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (ray) doesn't apply to mystic bolts, but a warlock can take Weapon Focus (mystic bolt) and apply it to both melee and ranged mystic bolts.~~~Creating a mystic bolt requires the hand to be free, but the bolt appears only briefly, so a warlock using mystic bolts has a free hand any time she isn't attacking with a mystic bolt. The warlock threatens with a mystic bolt, but only if she has a hand free." special="Because mystic bolts are impermanent, a spell that targets a single weapon (like ‹spell/magic weapon›) can't affect it, nor can a mystic bolt be made with magic weapon special abilities. Abilities that affect all weapon attacks the warlock makes, such as the *arcane striker* warlock talent, function with mystic bolts." repeat="The warlock chooses another damage type from the list above.~7~6" imp7="Each time she creates a mystic bolt, she can have it use any one of the damage types she has selected." replace="Vigilante specialization"}

::ab[Piercing Bolts (Ex)]{icon=touch-zap l3="The warlock can choose one mystic bolt per round to be a touch attack. This turns a melee mystic bolt into a melee touch attack or a ranged mystic bolt into a ranged touch attack." l5="The warlock can treat all of her mystic bolts as touch attacks." replace=Unshakable}
::ab[Warlock Talents]{head icon=power info="A warlock can select from any of the following vigilante talents, in addition to general ‹ability/vigilante talents›." alter="Vigilante talents"}

::ab[Arcane Striker (Su)]{next icon=power ability="The warlock gains ‹feat/Arcane Strike› as a bonus feat." imp12="When she activates Arcane Strike, she can choose to also give her weapons the ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, or ‹magic-enh/thundering› weapon special ability. She makes this choice each time she activates Arcane Strike, and it lasts for the same duration." imp16="She adds ‹magic-enh/anarchic›, ‹magic-enh/axiomatic›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/holy›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, and ‹magic-enh/unholy› to the list of special abilities she can select. She can choose to apply an alignment-based weapon special ability only while in her vigilante identity, and only if it matches the alignment of her vigilante identity."}

::ab[Elemental Armor (Su)]{next icon=def passive="When in her vigilante identity, the warlock gains armor made of elemental energy. She is surrounded by a nimbus of ice, lightning, or flames, granting her resistance 5 to cold, electricity, or fire, respectively. The damage type must be chosen when she selects this talent, and it can't be changed." imp4="The warlock's armor deals 1d6 points of damage of the chosen energy type to any creature striking the warlock with a natural weapon or a non-reach melee weapon. A creature that attempts a ‹rule/grapple› combat maneuver check against the warlock also takes this damage. A creature can take damage in this way no more than once per round." imp8="The energy resistance increases to 10." imp12="The damage increases to 2d6 points." imp16="The warlock becomes immune to the chosen damage type while in her vigilante identity."}

::ab[Familiar (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="The warlock gains a ‹sidekick/familiar›, using her vigilante level as her effective wizard level. The ‹ability/familiar› also has a social identity as a seemingly normal animal, though warlocks with outlandish familiars might still need to hide them."}

::ab[Social Simulacrum (Sp)]{next prereq="8th-level vigilante" icon=magic ability="The warlock can create a short-lived simulacrum (as ‹spell/lesser simulacrum›) that duplicates her social identity, allowing her to appear to be in two places at once. Creating the duplicate takes 1 hour and requires no material component cost. It lasts for 4 hours before turning back into an inert pile of snow.~~~The duplicate replicates only the warlock's social identity, with the same alignment and social talents. It has no magical abilities, and can't assume a vigilante identity. As with the spell, the simulacrum isn't under the warlock's control." special="The warlock can create a social simulacrum no more than once per day and can have only one in existence at a time."}

::ab[Tattoo Chamber (Su)]{next icon=magic flavor="This talent functions as the ‹arc-vigilante/cabalist› ability of the same name. The text below is from that ability." order=a2 ability="The warlock can magically absorb items through a tattoo on his hand or wrist and easily retrieve them or activate their magical abilities without making them manifest. The items seemingly disappear into the tattoo, but actually enter an extradimensional space that the warlock can access via the tattoo. Stowing an item in this way requires a @HLfull-round_action, and the space can store a number of items equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-third› of his vigilante level. These items must be items the warlock can hold in one hand." ability2="Retrieving a stowed item requires mentally activating the tattoo as a @HLswift_action. The item appears in the warlock's hand, so worn items must still be donned to gain their benefits." special="The warlock can also activate any ‹misc/spell-trigger› abilities of a stored item as though he were wielding the item, producing the magic effect from his tattoo rather than from the item. If the warlock dies while items are within his tattoo chamber, those items fade into existence within 5 feet of the corpse."}

wildsoul.name: Wildsoul
description: ## Wildsoul
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/61›

Within some vigilantes are incredible innate abilities that, although normally the domain of natural beasts, inexplicably aid them in fighting their enemies. These vigilantes have ties to the creature whose abilities they emulate, whether from a distant lycanthrope ancestor, a divine touch, unnatural experimentation, or something else altogether.

::ab[Natural Course (Ex)]{icon=power l1="When a wildsoul chooses a vigilante specialization, he must also choose a natural course: *arachnid, falconine, feline,* or *ursine.* This represents the type of creature that influences his body, granting him his remarkable abilities. Once this choice is made, it can't be changed.~~~At 2nd, 6th, 12th, and 18th levels, a wildsoul gains a specific ability tied to his natural course. These abilities count as vigilante talents, as onlookers who see the wildsoul in his social identity use them overtly might suspect him of being something more than he appears; the more obvious animalistic abilities (like *bear form* and *shoot webs*) impart a -10 penalty on the wildsoul's Disguise check to appear as his social identity." alter="Vigilante specialization" replace="Vigilante talents gained at 2nd, 6th, 12th, and 18th levels"}
::h3[Arachnid]{jl}
These wildsouls have the blood of spiders running through their veins.

::ab[Heightened Senses (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="An arachnid wildsoul can anticipate physical danger and react astonishingly quickly." ability="He gains the ‹vigtalent/stalker sense› vigilante talent, even if he is not a stalker."}

::ab[Shoot Webs (Ex)]{l=6 icon=zap ability="An arachnid wildsoul's wrists can produce thick, sticky webbing that he can shoot at his enemies. Shooting webbing is a ranged touch attack with a range increment of 10 feet. The webbing acts as a ‹eq-weapon/tanglefoot bag› when it hits a creature, except the DC of the Reflex save to avoid being glued to the floor or unable to fly is equal to 10 + 1/2 the arachnid wildsoul's vigilante level + his Constitution modifier. Like a tanglefoot bag, the webbing does not work underwater." useMod=Constitution3}

::ab[Web Specialist (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power order=am ability="An arachnid wildsoul can coat his hands and feet in super-sticky webbing, even over equipment such as gloves and boots. This gives him a climb speed of 30 feet." move="An arachnid wildsoul can use his webbing to create the equivalent of a 50-foot length of ‹eq-misc/silk rope<_50_ft› (hp 4, break DC 24) and can shoot it at any flat, unoccupied surface that is at least one 5-foot square in size. This provokes attacks of opportunity. If the arachnid wildsoul succeeds at a ranged attack against AC 10, the rope sticks fast to the surface, can only be removed if it is destroyed, and can support the weight of the arachnid wildsoul plus one additional Medium or smaller creature. If more creatures attempt to use the webbing rope, it is destroyed. This costs a daily use of *shoot webs.*"}

::ab[Web Master (Ex)]{l=18 icon=boost-power ability="An arachnid wildsoul can use *web specialist* without spending a daily use of *shoot webs.* Additionally, he can create numerous web-ropes as he is moving and swing from rope to rope, essentially allowing him to move at his base speed through the air as long as he always uses at least one hand to hold the webbing and continues to have tether points within 50 feet and above him. The web ropes he creates in this way dissolve as soon as he is done using them to swing."}
::h3[Falconine]{jl}
These wildsouls have the blood of falcons or other large birds of prey running through their veins.

::ab[Soft Landing (Ex)]{l=2 icon=magic ability="A falconine wildsoul can grow feathery wing-flaps under his arms that allow him to fall slowly. He gains the ‹vigtalent/perfect fall› vigilante talent."}
::ab[Eagle Eye (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost passive="A falconine wildsoul hones his visual senses. He gains a competence bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his vigilante level on visual Perception checks."}

::ab[Take to the Air (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power flavor="A falconine wildsoul can harness the flight inherent in his blood." ability="While in his vigilante identity, he has feathery wings that grant him a fly speed of 40 feet (with good maneuverability)."}

::ab[Deadly Dive (Ex)]{l=18 icon=melee flavor="A falconine wildsoul has learned how to mimic the swooping and diving of birds of prey." fullround="While he is flying at least 30 feet above the ground, a falconine wildsoul can dive onto a creature by moving up to twice his fly speed and make a single melee attack. The movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal, and the attack provokes an attack of opportunity from the attack's target. If the attack is successful, the falconine wildsoul deals the attack's damage plus 2d6 points of damage for every 10 feet he dove (to a maximum of 20d6 damage if he dove 100 feet or more). Even if the attack misses, the target still takes the extra damage for the distance of the dive, but it receives a Reflex save to halve the damage (DC = 10 + 1/2 the falconine wildsoul's vigilante level + his Strength or Dexterity modifier, whichever is higher).~~~A falconine vigilante can dive in this way a number of times per day equal to his Strength or Dexterity modifier, whichever is higher."}
::h3[Ursine]{jl}
These wildsouls have the blood of bears running through their veins.

::ab[Deadly Claws (Ex)]{l=2 icon=melee ability="An ursine wildsoul's hands become claws in his vigilante identity, granting him two claw attacks. These are primary ‹rule/natural attacks› that deal 1d4 points of damage if he is Medium or ‹umr/natural_attacks>«1d3 if he is Small›."}

::ab[Bear Jaws (Ex)]{l=6 icon=melee ability="An ursine wildsoul gains a bite attack while in his vigilante identity. This is a primary natural attack that deals 1d4 points of damage if he is Medium or 1d3 if he is Small."}

::ab[Thick Hide (Ex)]{l=12 icon=def passive="An ursine wildsoul in his vigilante identity has skin that bristles with thick hair like that of a bear's. He gains a natural armor bonus of +1." increment="This natural armor bonus~12~4"}

::ab[Bear Form (Su)]{l=18 icon=magic flavor="An ursine wildsoul has learned how to take a bear's form." standard="He can change into bear form at will, and the transformation lasts until he changes back. While transformed, he becomes a ‹monster/grizzly bear› as ‹spell/beast shape II›. An ursine wildsoul loses his ability to speak while in bear form and is limited to the sounds that a normal bear can make, though he can communicate normally with other bears, as ‹spell/speak with animals›."}
::h3[Feline]{jl}
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/7›
Feline wildsouls have the blood of great cats running through their veins.

::ab[Feline Attributes (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost ability="A feline wildsoul gains the ‹umr/low-light vision› and ‹umr/scent› special abilities and reduces any penalty he takes on Perception checks due to distance by an amount equal to ‹misc/half› his vigilante level (minimum 0). If he also gains low-light vision or scent from a feat or racial trait, he instead gains a +2 bonus on Perception checks for each ability he already has." special="He can take ‹feat/Aspect of the Beast›, ignoring the feat's prerequisites."}

::ab[Feline Grace (Ex)]{icon=def l6="A feline wildsoul gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge›, using his vigilante level as his effective ‹class/rogue› level." l12="He also gains ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge›."}

::ab[Feline Pounce (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power flavor="A feline wildsoul can strike quickly and lethally at a moment's notice." ability="He gains the ‹vigtalent/mad rush› avenger vigilante talent, even if he is not an avenger."}

::ab[Nine Lives (Su)]{l=18 icon=def flavor="A feline wildsoul can survive attacks that would kill lesser vigilantes." ability="He gains the ‹talent/defensive roll› advanced rogue talent and can use this ability eight times per day."}

zealot.name: Zealot
description: ## Zealot
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/62›
Dedicated to a single deity, the zealot hunts the enemies of his faith in secret, often because his religion is outlawed or persecuted in the region.
::cskill{gain=kr~spc lose=dd~ke~perf~soh}
::ab[Skill Ranks per Level]{icon=down info="A zealot gains a number of skill ranks equal to 4 + his Intelligence modifier at each level, instead of 6 + his Intelligence modifier skill ranks."}

::ab[Weapon Proficiencies]{icon=power ability="A zealot is also proficient in his deity's favored weapon, even if it is an exotic weapon, and if the deity's favored weapon is unarmed strike, he gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Spellcasting]{icon=learn ability="A zealot casts divine spells and orisons as an ‹class/inquisitor› and uses the ‹main/spells_>inquisitor«'s spell list›." replace="4th-, 8th-, 10th-, 14th-, and 16th-level vigilante talents"}

::ab[Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells]{icon=down info="Zealots can't cast spells of an alignment opposed to his vigilante identity's alignment or that of his deity. Spells associated with particular alignments are indicated by the chaotic, evil, good, and lawful descriptors in their spell descriptions."}

::ab[Zealot Talents]{head icon=power info="A zealot can select from any of the following vigilante talents, in addition to general ‹ability/vigilante talents›." alter="Vigilante talents"}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{next icon=aura ability="A zealot who takes this talent can ‹ability/channel energy› with an effective cleric level equal to his vigilante level - 4 (minimum 1) while in his vigilante identity. If good in his vigilante identity (or if he worships a good deity), the vigilante channels positive energy and can choose to deal damage to undead creatures or to heal living creatures. If evil in his vigilante identity (or if he worships an evil deity), the vigilante channels negative energy and can choose to deal damage to living creatures or to heal undead creatures. If the vigilante is neutral in his vigilante identity and worships a neutral deity, the vigilante must choose whether he channels positive or negative energy. Once this choice is made, it can't be reversed."}

::ab[Discern Lies (Sp)]{next icon=magic prereq="6th-level vigilante" immediate="The zealot can use ‹spell/discern lies› as a spell-like ability for a number of rounds per day equal to his vigilante level. The rounds need not be consecutive."}

::ab[Empower Symbol (Su)]{next prereq="6th-level vigilante" icon=magic standard="The zealot can empower his holy symbol. If the zealot is of evil alignment in his vigilante identity (or worships an evil deity), this works as if the holy symbol was the center of a ‹spell/desecrate› effect. If the zealot is neutral in his vigilante identity and worships a neutral deity, he can choose whether this ability acts as per consecrate or desecrate, but once the choice is made, it cannot be changed." imp10="The zealot treats his holy symbol as a permanent fixture for the purpose of determining the consecration or desecration effects."}

::ab[Stalwart (Ex)]{next prereq="12th-level vigilante" flavor="A zealot with this talent can use mental and physical resilience to avoid certain attacks." icon=def ability="If he succeeds at a Fortitude or Will saving throw against an attack that has a reduced effect on a successful save, he avoids the effect entirely. ::ab[Stern Gaze (Ex)]{next icon=boost flavor="A zealot with this talent is skilled at sensing deception and intimidating his foes." passive="The zealot gains a morale bonus on all Intimidate and Sense Motive checks equal to half his vigilante level (minimum +1)."}

::ab[Zealot Smite (Su)]{next icon=power-lower flavor="A zealot with this talent can use this power to smite a foe." useInc=vigilante~3~2 useF="2~1~3" order=nwu info="Zealots that are good in their vigilante identity (or worship a good deity) can *smite* evil creatures, those that are evil in their vigilante identity (or worship an evil deity) can *smite* good creatures, those that are chaotic in their vigilante identity (or worship a chaotic deity) can *smite* lawful creatures, and those that are lawful in their vigilante identity (or worship a lawful deity) can *smite* chaotic creatures. A zealot that is neutral when in his vigilante identity and that worships a neutral deity can pick to *smite* any one of the four alignments; other zealots must select from the alignments available based on their own alignment or their deity's. Once that choice is made, it can't be changed." swift="The zealot chooses one target within sight to *smite.* If this target is of the proper alignment, the zealot adds his Charisma bonus on his attack rolls (to a maximum of half his vigilante level) and his vigilante level on all damage rolls against the target of his *smite.* If the target of *smite* is an outsider with an alignment subtype of the *zealot's smite,* a similarly aligned dragon, or an undead creature (if the *zealot's smite* is against evil creatures), a good-aligned cleric or paladin (if the *zealot's smite* is against good creatures), a chaotic aberration (if the *zealot's smite* is against chaotic creatures), or a lawful-aligned cleric or paladin (if the *zealot's smite* is against lawful creatures), the bonus to damage on the first successful attack increases to 2 points of damage per vigilante level. Regardless of the target, the *zealot's smite* attacks bypass the creature's DR as if it were a weapon with a +5 enhancement bonus."}
